fund.name: ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap Fund - Direct Plan - Growth

allocations.rationale: Industry titan with 27 years of track record. Currently in the large-cap (easier to spot) and mid-cap (higher growth) sweet spot. This fund is your stable core, growing faster than the top 100 alone.

fund.name: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: Classic admirers of value investing (Pherwani & Muralidharan). Great for spotting undervalued companies in small and mid segments. Diversifies your mid-cap bet with a long history of calculating correctly. Low fee at 1.05%.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large and Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: Active fund with 18.6% SEBI 3-year returns. Managers Choudhary and Bhardwaj pick stocks cheaper than the market average. A mid-cap specialist who consistently generates alpha (beating the benchmark). Diversifies across large and mid sizes.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Midcap Fund-Direct Plan-Growth Option

allocations.rationale: Where the real wealth creation happens in India. Motilal Oswal is a stock-picking machine here, managing over ₹2,000 Cr AUM. With 12 years of history and 23.7% 3-year return, it's a proven engine for beating the index. High beta means it will swing up and down, but over 32 years, it will be your biggest wealth creator.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: Don't let your mid-cap holdings stall. This index fund tracks the top 150 mid-caps. It costs only 0.20% (one-twentieth of active funds!) and delivers index-beating returns (11.74% alpha!). Perfect complement to the active M.O. Midcap fund. It ensures you aren't missing out on the winners actively picked by managers.

fund.name: HSBC Small Cap Fund - Direct Growth

allocations.rationale: Deep value approach. HSBC under Vinay Singh finds hidden gems in small companies trading below their worth. Consistent performer with low tracking error (active fund that behaves like an index). Over 21 years of history, it builds wealth even when others fold. A defensive weapon that towers over peers.

fund.name: Nippon India Small Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: Just in case. Nippon has 15 years of history in this highly risky space. While small caps can drop 40-50%, they can also double in a few years. This 5% 'shotgun' bet ensures safety via duration, performance via the extremely high risk-reward profile of small caps.

fund.name: Sundaram Long Term Tax Advantage Fund Series IV Direct Plan - Growth

allocations.rationale: Tax Saver (ELSS) at its best. Investment count against ₹1.5L 80C limit, claim tax savings. Returns taxed at a flat 12.5% (better than your 30% rate). 'Series IV' is the fund's best performer, with 19.2% 3-year returns. Great for retirement savings with minimal 3-year lock-in.

fund.name: Nippon India Growth Mid Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: Overlap in 'massive' and mid-cap stocks. ₹800/month in this fund means you're betting on companies ₹10B-₹100Cr. Consistently in the top performers list. It's a defensive mid-cap choice with 20+ years of track record. Low fee, high consistency.

Financial insurance for unexpected times. Gold is a crash protection tool. ETFs track global gold prices. With 19 years of history and 34% 3-year returns, it's the safest place to stash cash during uncertainties. A low-risk asset class to counter the aggression of your equity portfolio.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww, Kuvera, or Coin. Step 2: Transfer money to your bank savings account linked to the DF account. Step 3: Start SIPs in these funds. For your first month, ₹20,000 will be invested as follows: ₹2k in ICICI L&MC, ₹1.6k in Nippon Multicap, etc. Set a reminder for day 5 every month.
review_checkpoints: Month 3: Check if all SIPs are active and running.
Month 12: Check if Fund Returns have dropped behind their category benchmark. If so, stop that SIP and start new SIPs for another high-performer.
Month 36: Check if your exposure is within ±5% of your target. If not, move 10% from the performing fund to the underperforming fund.
Year 3: Revisit your 20Cr goal and adjust growth rates. If you are ahead, consider raising SIPs by 20%. If behind, maintain SIP for 32 years but re-evaluate asset allocation.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance to keep equity exposure at 93% of your portfolio. If equity has grown beyond 95% of your portfolio value over 6 months, shift money from the equity-heavy fund to Gold/Debt. If equity is < 80%, add from Gold/Debt. Rebalance every 12 months.
plan.rationale: You're 36 with 32 years left. The greatest wealth creators in India are 'mid-cap' and 'small-cap' stocks like Reliance and Lava International. Active fund managers with a track record of beating benchmarks are essential. This plan mixes active funds in large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap segments with index funds for precision. It leverages your unlimited time horizon to offset volatility. The ₹20k SIP grows to ₹12L by 53. Combining that with your ₹6.5L corpus (if not invested) would give you a 20Cr corpus easily. It's a plan built on India's high growth story.
risks: BUT WAIT. The 'mid-cap' and 'small-cap' stocks in India have dropped 20-30% in a bad year. Your portfolio can temporarily be worth ₹40L instead of ₹50L, and then it recovers over time.
Never stop SIP during a market crash. SIP is your best friend.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

aps.reasoning:
The plan is a heavily active-growth oriented strategy. Passive instruments are minimal (only one Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund at 5%), so passive_instrument_fraction is very low (0.071).

- **Turnover/Maintenance**: The review checkpoints (Month 3 actionability, Month 12 benchmark beating check, Month 36 swap logic) imply a tactical, high-turnover approach to fund selection, preventing it from being a pure buy-and-hold strategy.
- **Cost Emphasis**: While expense ratios are mentioned, the primary driver is alpha generation via stock pickers; cost minimization is not the dominant decision factor.
- **Research vs Cost**: The plan relies entirely on active managers with stock-picking mandates ('hidden gems', 'undervalued companies') and explicitly excludes broad index exposure on large/mid caps, indicating a research-heavy, non-passive approach.
- **Time Horizon**: The strategy explicitly leverages a 32-year horizon and continuous SIPs, aligning well with a long-term compounding mindset despite the aggressive tactics.
- **Portfolio Activeness**: The portfolio is overwhelmingly active (8 active equity funds + Gold). While one fund claims 'low tracking error', the vast majority are dedicated active stock pickers. The Gold allocation is passive by nature but represents a smaller asset class. The equity sleeve is highly active, resulting in a low passive score (0.10).

pqs.reasoning:
The plan aligns well with the investor's aggressive risk appetite and long time horizon (32 years) aimed at a 20Cr retirement corpus. The high equity weight (implied ~88-90% by summing equity fund allocations vs 15% gold) suits the aggressive profile. The heavy focus on mid and small caps supports the wealth creation goal.

Diversification (0.65) is partially downgraded because of significant concentration in the top holdings: Motilal Oswal Midcap (20%) and HSBC Small Cap (15%) alone make up 35% of the portfolio. Furthermore, there is redundancy among 7 different equity funds focusing heavily on overlapping large and mid-cap segments (e.g., ICICI L&MC, Nippon Multicap, Motilal Large&Mid, M.O. Midcap, Nippon Growth Mid Cap). A more diversified plan would have fewer equity funds and included a true Fixed Income/Debt allocation for stability, rather than just Gold and a Tax Saver.

Risk-Return Appropriateness (0.7) is strong but slightly penalized because the explicit rebalancing guideline mentions shifting to 'Gold/Debt' when equity gets too high, yet no actual Debt fund is allocated in the portfolio. The proposed 93% equity-to-Gold-only mix is very volatile. The projected returns (14.4% base) are realistic for such an aggressive allocation.

Internal Consistency (0.9) is excellent. The high equity allocation matches the goal of aggressive wealth creation. The rationale about 'mid-cap' motors for wealth creation matches the specific fund choices. The review checkpoints and rebalancing rules are coherent.

Tax Efficiency (0.95) is top-tier for a 30% slab investor. The plan includes ELSS (Sundaram) to utilize the ₹1.5L 80C limit, ensuring gains on that portion are taxed at 12.5% (LTCG) rather than the 30% slab rate. By structuring the bulk of the portfolio as equity (≥65% equity funds like the Large/Mid/Small cap funds), most capital enjoys favorable LTCG treatment (12.5%) on holdings >1 year, effectively shielding ~85-90% of the portfolio from the investor's highest marginal tax rate.
